draft.content_markdown:
# [anti_pattern] Prune duplicate remedies after no-gain failure cycles
## Signal
Repeated attempts to fix the same class of problem create multiple overlapping remedies, but measured outcomes do not improve.

## Practice
Do not keep adding narrowly scoped, failure-specific fixes when they duplicate an existing capability and produce no measurable gain. Instead, pause after a no-improvement cycle, compare the remedies for overlap, merge them into one clear general procedure, and retire redundant variants to reduce context clutter and maintenance cost. What not to repeat: accumulating near-identical “special case” fixes just because earlier attempts failed.

## Contraindications
Do not apply this pruning step when the variants address genuinely different operating conditions, have distinct evidence of effectiveness, or must remain separate for audit, safety, or compatibility reasons.

## Evidence
